title: Incorrect query result when a LEFT JOIN subquery is flattened
poc:
PRAGMA automatic_index=OFF".
CREATE TABLE t1(id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY);
CREATE TABLE t2(id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, c2 INTEGER);
CREATE TABLE t3(id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, c3 INTEGER);

SELECT t1.id, x2.id, x3.id
FROM t1
LEFT JOIN (SELECT * FROM t2) AS x2 ON t1.id=x2.c2
LEFT JOIN t3 AS x3 ON x2.id=x3.c3;
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t1(id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT NOT NULL);
CREATE TABLE t2(id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, c2 INTEGER, value REAL DEFAULT 0.0);
CREATE TABLE t3(id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, c3 INTEGER, description TEXT);
INSERT INTO t1(id, name) VALUES(456, 'Test Name');
INSERT INTO t2(id, c2, value) VALUES(1, NULL, 3.14), (2, 456, NULL);
INSERT INTO t3(id, c3, description) VALUES(1, 1, 'First'), (2, 2, 'Second');
SELECT t1.id, x2.id, x3.id FROM t1 LEFT JOIN (SELECT * FROM t2 WHERE value IS NOT NULL) AS x2 ON t1.id=x2.c2 LEFT JOIN t3 AS x3 ON x2.id=x3.c3;
